name: ApiDocInfo
documentation:
Adds top-level metadata to generated API documentation.

Place this annotation on a package or handler type to describe the generated API document itself. Compiler options such as {@code -Afluxzero.openapi.title=...} can still override the same values for annotation-processor output.

methods.name: apiReferencePath
methods.documentation:
Path where the generated API reference HTML page is served when {@link #serveApiReference()} is enabled.

Relative paths are resolved against the {@link Path} value at the same package or handler type where this annotation is placed. Absolute paths start at the application root.

methods.name: apiReferenceRenderer
methods.documentation: Renderer used for the generated API reference HTML page.

methods.name: apiReferenceScriptUrl
methods.documentation:
Optional renderer script URL. Leave empty to use the SDK default for {@link #apiReferenceRenderer()}.

Set this to a self-hosted URL when CDN access is not desired.

methods.name: apiReferenceStylesheetUrl
methods.documentation:
Optional renderer stylesheet URL. Leave empty to use the SDK default for {@link #apiReferenceRenderer()} when it has one.

This is mainly useful for Swagger UI.

methods.name: components
methods.documentation:
Extra OpenAPI components such as shared responses or security schemes. Component paths are relative to the
 OpenAPI {@code components} object, for example {@code responses.error}.

methods.name: contactEmail
methods.documentation: Contact email address.

methods.name: contactName
methods.documentation: Contact name.

methods.name: contactUrl
methods.documentation: Contact URL.

methods.name: description
methods.documentation: API description. Markdown is allowed by OpenAPI tooling.

methods.name: extensions
methods.documentation:
Top-level OpenAPI vendor extensions as {@code name=json} entries, for example
 {@code x-code-samples-enabled=true}. Values are parsed as JSON when possible and otherwise rendered as strings.

methods.name: licenseName
methods.documentation: License name.

methods.name: licenseUrl
methods.documentation: License URL.

methods.name: logoAltText
methods.documentation: Optional logo alt text, rendered as {@code info.x-logo.altText}.

methods.name: logoUrl
methods.documentation: Optional logo URL, rendered as the common {@code info.x-logo.url} vendor extension.

methods.name: openApiPath
methods.documentation:
Path where the generated OpenAPI document is served when {@link #serveOpenApi()} is enabled.

This endpoint is also served when {@link #serveApiReference()} is enabled, because the generated HTML reference page needs an OpenAPI document to render.

Relative paths are resolved against the {@link Path} value at the same package or handler type where this annotation is placed. Absolute paths start at the application root.

methods.name: openApiVersion
methods.documentation: OpenAPI document version. Defaults to {@code 3.0.1}; set {@code 3.1.0} when a consumer requires OpenAPI 3.1.

methods.name: security
methods.documentation:
Top-level OpenAPI security requirements.

Values are rendered as security requirement objects. Use {@code bearerAuth} for a scheme without scopes, or {@code oauth2=read,write} for scoped schemes. Define the actual schemes through {@link #components()} with paths such as {@code securitySchemes.bearerAuth}.

methods.name: serveApiReference
methods.documentation:
If {@code true}, a small HTML API reference endpoint is served for the generated OpenAPI document.

The endpoint uses {@link #apiReferenceRenderer()} and references externally hosted or self-hosted renderer assets. The SDK does not bundle Redoc, Scalar, Swagger UI, or other frontend assets.

methods.name: serveOpenApi
methods.documentation:
If {@code true}, the generated OpenAPI document is served as an automatic web endpoint.

The endpoint is only registered for handlers in the annotated package or type. Use {@link #openApiPath()} to avoid route conflicts with application endpoints.

methods.name: servers
methods.documentation: API servers.

methods.name: termsOfService
methods.documentation: Terms of service URL.

methods.name: title
methods.documentation: API title.

methods.name: version
methods.documentation: API version.
